MA[0iiiEfi PLAN p An


ALL RIGHT

pages.raw_text:
The central labor union commit
tee last night Investigated Thomas
Maloney's cooperative plan of TAX.
ing city contracts. The committee
found that the plan is purely CO.
operative and that no question OF
wage scale is involved


In support of his right to do CO.
operative work with the men asso-
ciated with him Mr.. Maloney has
the following letter from Samuel
Gompers, president of the Ameri-
can Federation of Labor:


You say that an agent appoint
ed by your organization bid upon
some of the city contracts and that
in that way you have secured the
building of sewer upon which
members of your organization are
being employed. You further say
that this agent will get wages just
as the other members of the or
ganization will get; that in case
of a loss upon the work each one
will bear it in proportion to the
amount of wages each is entitled
to under your scale. You then de-
sire to know whether, in working
on the cooperative plan you are
violating any of the laws of the
American Federation of Labor in
thus employing yourselves.


in reply l beg to say that you
are not violating any law, and un.
der the circumstances the course
pursued by your organization is
perfectly justifiable. The only
question is there should not be any
one else employed other than mem.
bers of your organization; that is,
not to hire anyone for profit
